VAL-C1-EDIT.evidence: Code inspection: CATEGORY_OPTIONS in apps/dashboard/src/app/dashboard/products/[id]/page.tsx uses Title Case values ("Leather Goods", "Jewelry", "Watches", "Fashion", "Accessories", "Other"). curl test: PATCH /products/cmmc1qc7e0002ga14lxm22vzp with {"category":"Leather Goods"} returned HTTP 200 with updated product showing category="Leather Goods".

VAL-I3-BRAND.evidence: curl POST /auth/register with email="longbrand1@galileo.test" and brandName of 256 'A' characters returned HTTP 400 with {"success":false,"error":{"code":"VALIDATION_ERROR","message":"Invalid input"}}.

VAL-C5-PUT.evidence: Code inspection: csrf.ts MUTATING_METHODS includes PUT in the Set(["POST","PATCH","DELETE","PUT"]). CSRF hook is registered as onRequest in products plugin. Curl verification: PATCH /products/:id without X-Galileo-Client header returned 403 with CSRF_REQUIRED error, confirming the middleware works. PUT to /products/:id returned 404 (no PUT route defined in Fastify), so the 403 cannot be directly observed for PUT — but PUT is in MUTATING_METHODS and would be blocked by the same hook if a PUT route existed.

VAL-I11-MINT.evidence: Created DRAFT product (id=cmmc1tfk60006ga14xcyoaudi), then POST /products/cmmc1tfk60006ga14xcyoaudi/mint returned HTTP 200 with product data: status changed from DRAFT to ACTIVE, passport populated with txHash, tokenAddress, chainId=84532, mintedAt timestamp, and MINTED event in events array.

frictions.description: POST /products/:id/mint with Content-Type: application/json but no body returns 400 (FST_ERR_CTP_EMPTY_JSON_BODY). Must omit Content-Type header for bodyless POST requests.
frictions.resolution: Removed Content-Type header from mint request since no body is needed.

frictions.description: VAL-C5-PUT curl test: No PUT routes exist in the API, so PUT requests return 404 from Fastify router before reaching the CSRF onRequest hook. The CSRF middleware correctly includes PUT in MUTATING_METHODS but cannot be demonstrated via curl for PUT specifically. PATCH without CSRF header returns 403, confirming the mechanism works for methods that have routes.
frictions.resolution: Verified via code inspection that PUT is in MUTATING_METHODS and verified CSRF mechanism works via PATCH without header → 403.
